segments.text: About this time Hezekiah fell very sick and was about to die. The prophet Isaiah, son of Amoz, went to him and said, {
This is what the Lord says: Put your affairs in order, because you are going to die. You won't recover.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: When Hezekiah heard this, he went to pray privately to the Lord, saying
Please remember Lord how I have followed you faithfully with all my heart. I have done what is good in your sight.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Then Hezekiah cried and cried.
Then the Lord sent a message to Isaiah, saying,
Go and tell Hezekiah, This is what the Lord, the God of your forefather David, says: I have heard your prayer, I have seen your tears. I will add fifteen years to your life.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: I will save you and this city from the king of Assyria. I will defend this city.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: This is the sign from the Lord to you that the Lord will do what he promised:
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Look, I will make the shadow made by the sun go back the ten steps that it has gone down on the stairway of Ahaz. So the sun went back the ten steps that it had gone down on the stairway of Ahaz.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: This is what Hezekiah, king of Judah, wrote after he recovered from his sickness:
I said to myself, {
Do I have to go to my death just as my life is going well? Why can't I count on the rest of my years?
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: I said, {
I will never again see the Lord, the Lord, in the land of the living. I won't see anyone else again, none of the inhabitants of this world.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Like a shepherd's tent, the place where I live has been pulled up and taken away from me. Like a weaver I have rolled up the cloth of my life and cut it from the loom. Day and night you bring me to an end.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: I lie there patiently until the morning, but I feel like there's a lion breaking every bone in my body. Day and night you bring me to an end.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: I scream like a swift or a songbird,  I moan like a dove. My eyes grow dim as I look heavenwards. I'm being attacked, Lord, please come and support me!
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Yet what can I say? He told me what was going to happen, and he himself did it.  I will walk quietly for the rest of my life because of the painful experience I went through.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Lord, we live by what you say and do, and I find life in all of this. You have given me back my health and allowed me to live.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: It was definitely for my own good I went through this bitter experience. You in your love saved me from the pit of destruction and you have forgiven all my sins.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Those in the grave cannot praise you, the dead cannot praise you. Those who go down into the pit can no longer hope in your faithfulness.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: It's only the living who can praise you as I'm doing today. Parents explain to their children how you can be trusted.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: The Lord saved me! We will sing my songs with stringed instruments all the days of our lives in the Lord's Temple.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Isaiah had said, {
Prepare a dressing of figs and spread it on the skin sores so he may recover.
segments.speaker: Isaiah had

segments.text: Hezekiah had asked, {
What is the sign to confirm that I will go to the Lord's Temple?
segments.speaker: Hezekiah had
